question_text: State the formula for calculating pressure and identify what each symbol represents.
correct_answer: P = F/A, where P is pressure (measured in Pascals or N/m²), F is force (measured in Newtons), and A is area (measured in square metres).
explanation: The pressure formula shows that pressure increases when force increases or when the area decreases. This explains why sharp objects create higher pressure than blunt ones with the same force.
